claim: Context engineering—strategically choosing what the model sees—is more important than prompt engineering for agent reliability
actionable: Shift your focus from optimizing prompts to optimizing what data reaches the model; design which information is visible vs. hidden based on task relevance
source_title: How we solved Context Management in Agents

claim: Simple truncation breaks reasoning; the model forgets context and follow-ups feel like new conversations
actionable: Do not use naive truncation (keeping only the first N characters) for managing context; you will lose reasoning continuity and break multi-turn interactions
source_title: How we solved Context Management in Agents

claim: LLM-driven summarization is unreliable for context reduction; the model gets too much control over what is preserved and what is lost
actionable: Avoid letting the model summarize its own context for pruning; instead use deterministic head/tail preservation with an external memory store
source_title: How we solved Context Management in Agents

claim: Smart truncation (keep head + tail, store middle in memory) + retrievable memory store works reliably for managing context in long-running agents
actionable: Implement head/tail preservation (e.g., keep first 100 and last 100 tokens) and store the middle in a memory system the agent can query at any time
source_title: How we solved Context Management in Agents

claim: Long session evals (testing agent performance after 10+ turns) expose context management bugs that don't appear in short sessions
actionable: Create evals that test agents over 10+ turn conversations; this will surface context-related failures that short-session tests miss
source_title: How we solved Context Management in Agents

claim: Sub-agents for data-heavy tasks keep the main agent's context light; offload complex searches, summaries, or analysis to specialized agents
actionable: When a single agent's context grows too large, delegate heavy data-processing tasks to sub-agents that operate independently and return summaries to the main agent
source_title: How we solved Context Management in Agents

claim: Long-term memory (persistent across sessions) is still unsolved; agents currently only have in-session memory and a retrieval mechanism
actionable: Be aware that agents cannot truly remember facts across separate conversation sessions without explicit persistent memory implementation; build it yourself or it does not exist
source_title: How we solved Context Management in Agents
